author: Tyler Ortolano
content:
Ottawa-area Automotive Mechanics! Looking to get off the shop floor and into a role where every day is a little different?

We’re currently hiring a Field Service Technician to work on industrial equipment with a well-established organization that invests heavily in training and long-term career growth.

This opportunity offers:
• Strong job stability in a high-demand industry
• Competitive salary and great benefits
• Annual tool and boot allowance
• Company service truck and gas card
• Ongoing technical training and advancement opportunities

If you’re a licensed 310S mechanic and curious about applying your skills in a new environment, it might be worth a quick conversation.

Feel free to message Shawn Clark directly for more details.

hashtag#skilledtrades hashtag#hiring hashtag#310S hashtag#certustechnicalsearch hashtag#ottawa hashtag#ottawajobs

A quick market check will tell you that finding skilled Truck, Trailer, and Heavy Equipment Mechanics isn’t getting any easier. There’s a shortage across the country, and no single company is going to 'solve' that overnight.

As an employer, it’s worth asking:
• Are we fairly compensating our mechanics, including offering strong shift premiums for afternoons/nights?
• Have we created a positive and welcoming culture for new employees?
• Is our shop organized and are parts ready when the unit hits the bay?
• Can our employees continue to grow and develop within their trade?
• What truly sets us apart as an employer of choice?

The shops that are still landing good techs aren’t necessarily the ones with the flashiest ads, they’re the ones making the day-to-day job better, because the industry talks.

If you’re a skilled mechanic reading this and realizing you’re not getting this from your current employer, then we should talk. Happy to share who we’re working with...no pressure.

Certus Technical Search

content:
Certus Technical Search is hiring a senior Dock & Door Technician to support the continued growth of a well-established service team in the Greater Edmonton area.

This is a field-based role for someone who knows the dock & door space and is looking to play a bigger part as the division continues to build out locally. You’ll be the face of service at customer sites: diagnosing issues, completing repairs, and helping maintain strong client relationships across the region.

What’s on the table:
•        $80,000 - $90,000 salary plus paid O/T
•        Corporate vehicle & gas card
•        iPhone and iPad provided
•        Monday–Friday, customer site service focused
•        Excellent benefits, pension plan and paid time off
•        Long-term runway as the Edmonton division continues to grow

This role requires:
•        Proven dock & door service experience (industry background required)
•        Strong mechanical and electrical troubleshooting ability
•        Comfortable working independently in the field
•        Confident communicating directly with customers
•        Valid driver’s license and clean abstract
•        Own basic set of tools

If you’re an experienced dock & door tech who takes pride in your work and wants a role with real autonomy, this is worth a confidential conversation.

Reach out to Shawn Clark for details.

author: Tyler Ortolano
content:
For the 310T’s in my network. What actually makes you leave a shop?
Not the ‘nice’ answer… the real one.

Is it:
·      Pay not keeping up
·      Management that doesn’t get it
·      Getting stuck on the worst jobs every day
·      Shift work / no work-life balance
·      Shop environment (tools, cleanliness, how you’re treated)
·      No room to move up or learn anything new

Or something else? 

Drop it in the comments. We’re curious to hear the straight goods!

At Certus Technical Search, we’re seeing continued demand across the Skilled Trades market. We’re actively recruiting across Canada for experienced:

 • Truck & Trailer Technicians
 • Heavy Equipment Mechanics
 • Automotive Technicians
 • Industrial / Maintenance Mechanics

Opportunities available across all shifts with a mix of union and non-union environments, offering competitive compensation and benefits, RRSP matching, tool & boot allowances, and long-term growth.

DM us directly to learn more.

author: Tyler Ortolano

content:
Automotive Technicians – are you curious about the industrial equipment side of the trade?

Good auto techs are always busy, however, many start to ask “Is there something out there with a bit more stability… or variety?”, and honestly, the move isn’t as big of a leap as people think.

Strong automotive techs already bring a lot of what industrial employers need including: solid diagnostic ability, electrical troubleshooting, preventative maintenance mindset, and the ability to work through problems without constant supervision.

Where I see the shift making sense is usually around quality of life. Many of the techs I speak with are looking for things like steadier hours vs. flat rate swings, more variety in the work, field exposure and independence, stronger long-term earning progression, environments that feel less production-line and more technical.

It’s not the right move for everyone, but for the right automotive technician, the industrial equipment world (forklifts, dock & door, compact heavy equipment, etc.) can be a very natural transition.

If you’re an automotive tech who’s been quietly wondering what else is out there, my inbox is open. Happy to share what I’m seeing in the market.

Certus Technical Search

author: Tyler Ortolano

content:
Truck & Trailer Mechanics Across Canada…The Market Is Evolving

If you’ve been in the trade for a while, you’ve likely noticed some steady shifts over the past few years.

Across Canada, licensed Truck & Trailer Mechanics, particularly Red Seal technicians with strong diagnostic capability continue to be in high demand. Fleets are growing, equipment is becoming more advanced, and the need for skilled, reliable technicians remains critical to keeping freight moving.
On the shop floor, the conversation is gradually evolving.

Experienced techs are paying closer attention to the full picture, not just compensation, but also shop organization, leadership support, scheduling, and long-term stability. At the same time, many employers are investing more intentionally in their maintenance environments, training programs, and team structures to stay competitive in a tight labour market.

One thing we continue to see across the Canadian market:

Most strong mechanics are not actively job searching. They’re busy, they’re productive, and many have built long tenures with their current teams. But they are open to hearing about well-run operations that offer the right mix of stability, support, and opportunity.

For mechanics, it’s a reminder that the market remains active for skilled professionals who take pride in their work.

For employers, it’s an opportunity to continue building shops and teams that attract and retain top technical talent.

The shops that tend to stand out today are the ones that combine solid leadership, organized operations, and a genuine investment in their technicians’ long-term success.

Shawn Clark
Senior Technical Recruitment Consultant
Certus Technical Search
